conjecture_title: Open problem on $\eta$-boundedness for disjoint union of two stars
paper_title: Hitting all maximum stable sets in $P_5$-free graphs
summary: The open problem of whether $(S_a \cup S_b)$-free graphs are $\eta$-bounded for all $a,b \geq 1$ remains unresolved. No paper resolving this specific conjecture was found in the indexed literature. A related paper (arXiv:2403.19737) resolves a different conjecture from the same source paper, concerning graphs without large induced matchings, confirming activity in this area but leaving the disjoint-union-of-stars question open.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper confirmed as 'A simple layered-wheel-like construction' by Chudnovsky, Fischer, Hajebi, Spirkl, Walczak. It provides a counterexample to a conjecture attributed to Trotignon (Conjecture 1.5 in some paper) about treewidth and induced subgraphs. It does not discuss η-boundedness, disjoint union of two stars, or the open problem reviewed here. The fuzz match was a false positive driven by shared authors.

search_queries: eta-bounded star-free graphs disjoint union two stars 2302.04986 Hajebi Spirkl
η-boundedness Sa∪Sb-free graphs stable sets hitting conjecture graph theory
notes: arXiv:2403.19737 ('Piercing independent sets in graphs without large induced matching') resolves a different conjecture from the same source paper (about $2K_2$-free or bounded induced matching graphs), not the disjoint-union-of-stars η-boundedness question. The source paper itself notes the analogy with the Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture: for χ-boundedness, H-free classes reduce to component-free classes, but the analogous reduction for η-boundedness (Conjecture 1.8) is unknown. No follow-up found in the indexed literature for the specific open problem.
